item.title: Turn On & Off Terminal Echoing
item.description: Echo is a feature that outputs any key pressed to the terminal and allows you to see what you are typing.Usually, this is useful in canonical mode; However, it could be a hassle if you are trying to hide the characters you are typing in the terminal or building complex user interfaces. Therefore, in this writeup, I will guide you step by step to help you turn on & off Echoing.

item.title: Save time with these Linux Shell commands
item.description: Here I put the list of Linux shell commands I use frequently and I find efficient and good to know sudo !! (or sudo bang bang): A lot of the time, when I copy a command, paste in shell and quickly press Enter, I realize I forgot to write sudo first. No worries, sudo !! rerun the last command with sudo prepended to it. Cut a word at a time backwards: Sometimes you want to remove a word backwrods in your command.

item.title: In C++, ++i is faster than i++
item.description: When I first started using C++, almost all sources use i++ inside a for-loop, until one day, I saw a loop with ++i and decided to investigate the differences between the two in terms of efficiency. Therefore, I am here to discuss what I found. Let’s take an example for better understanding: int i = 0 ; int j = i++; // j here is equal to 0 still because the increment happens after the return of the value // This means that the old value is kept in a copy.

item.title: Contact
item.description: Find Me on the Web Twitter Github Email Email: mlamine[dot]abdellaoui[at]aucegypt[dot]edu

item.title: Now
item.description: I survived death twice, so that has taught me alot about life. I am all about serving people and doing the things that will help change the world to a better place. I love numbers, just like Paul Erdős, and I wish to serve the world using technology the way Alan Turing did. I am open to kick start my career in tech. If you want to know more about me, I would be happy to chat with you

item.title: Now
item.description: I survived death twice, so that has taught me alot about life. I am all about serving people and doing the things that will make the world to a better place. I love numbers, just like Paul Erdős, and I wish to serve the world using technology the way Alan Turing did. I am open to kick start my career as a software engineer, site reliability engineer, software tester, or a data analyst.

item.title: Projects
item.description: Here you can find a list of my projects: Scrape CDC Vaccination data A git-scraping based project to track live vaccination data from the CDC website and push it into a JSON file. The Process is automated using a cron job and github actions. Source : cdc-vaccination-scraped Stack :YAML, Github Actions. amazon_track A python script that uses web scraping to track an Amazon product’s price and email the customer in case the price has gone below the threshold they set.

item.title: Resources
item.description: Github has this beautiful feature called Explore and amongst the things I like about this feature: The Awesome list, where one could find hundreds of github repos about all sorts of things: jobs, books, courses. This inspired me to create my Awesome list here. Courses Learn Python, Data Viz, Pandas & More Linux Foundation Free Courses Tech Blogs & resources I like My favourite Cpp blogger is by far Jason Turner and he, also, has a Youtube channel where he talks about Cpp as well.
